channel.title: Vynix Blog
channel.description: Practical guides on bug reporting, developer context, and shipping with AI coding agents.

item.title: How to Write Bug Reports AI Agents Can Fix
item.description: Learn how to write bug reports with UI evidence, logs, repro steps, and clear prompts so AI coding agents can actually ship fixes.

item.title: How Vynix cuts the cost of running AI coding agents
item.description: See how Vynix gives AI coding agents precise bug context so they waste fewer runs, use shorter prompts, and cost less to operate.

item.title: The context an AI agent needs to fix front-end bugs
item.description: Learn what front-end bug context an AI coding agent needs: DOM element, screenshot, console logs, network calls, reproduction notes, and scope.

item.title: What a feedback layer is, and why AI teams need one
item.description: Learn what a feedback layer is, why AI coding workflows need one, and how Vynix turns website bugs into actionable developer context.

item.title: Turn a visual note into a GitHub issue in one step
item.description: Show how Vynix turns a clicked UI problem into a GitHub issue with screenshot, element data, logs, network context, and AI diagnosis.

item.title: How Vynix Captures Console and Network Detail
item.description: See how Vynix turns a clicked UI bug into useful console, network, screenshot, element, and AI diagnosis context for developers.

item.title: Using the Vynix MCP Server With Your AI Agent
item.description: Learn how to use Vynix context with an AI coding agent so bug reports include the element, screenshot, logs, and likely cause.

item.title: From a Reported Bug to a Merged Fix
item.description: See a practical workflow for taking a vague website bug report, capturing page context with Vynix, and turning it into a reviewed, merged fix.

item.title: Collecting Website Feedback From Clients and Teammates
item.description: A practical guide to collecting useful website feedback with screenshots, technical context, clear ownership, and fewer back-and-forth messages.

item.title: How Vynix Works, From a Click to a Fix
item.description: See how Vynix captures element, screenshot, console, network context, and AI diagnosis to turn website bugs into build-ready work.

item.title: Who Vynix is for, and what it fixes
item.description: See who should use Vynix, from founders to QA teams, and how it turns website bugs into useful developer context.

item.title: Point and click feedback on any web page
item.description: Use Vynix to click broken UI, capture page context, and turn feedback into a GitHub issue or coding-agent prompt.

item.title: Reading the Vynix AI diagnosis and the files it finds
item.description: Learn how to read a Vynix AI diagnosis, check the evidence, and turn file hints into a clear fix plan or GitHub issue.

item.title: Review rounds: turn notes into a short fix list
item.description: Use Vynix to turn scattered website review comments into clear, contextual fixes developers and coding agents can act on.

item.title: Region and Element Screenshots in Vynix
item.description: Learn how Vynix uses region and element screenshots to turn visual bugs into clear developer context, prompts, and GitHub issues.

item.title: Projects, roles and sharing in Vynix
item.description: Learn how to organize Vynix projects, share annotated issues, and give developers the context they need to fix site bugs faster.

item.title: How to install Vynix on any website
item.description: Add Vynix to your site, verify the widget, and capture useful bug context from real pages without changing your workflow.

item.title: How to Connect GitHub and Hand Work to Copilot
item.description: Connect Vynix to GitHub, turn annotated website bugs into clear issues, and assign the right work to Copilot without losing context.

item.title: Vynix plans, and how to pick the right one
item.description: Compare Vynix plan types at a high level, from the free plan to paid options, and choose based on your debugging workflow.

item.title: What we are building next at Vynix
item.description: A practical look at Vynix's direction: deeper agent workflows, richer feedback context, broader integrations, and where to track shipped updates.
